title: Process for preparing 2-exo-(2-methylbenzyloxy)-1-methyl-4-isopropyl-7-oxabicyclo[2.2.1]heptane
abstract: This invention relates to a process for preparing (Â±)-2-exo-(2-Methylbenzyloxy)-1-methyl-4- isopropyl-7-oxabicyclo[2.2.1]heptane of the formula (I), any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof, comprising the steps of (a) reacting (Â±)-2-exo-hydroxy-1-methyl-4-isopropyl-7-oxabicyclo[2.2.1]heptane of the formula (II), any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof (as defined in the description) with elemental sodium to form a sodium salt of the formula (III), any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof (as defined in the description), and (b) reacting said sodium salt (III) with a 2-Methylbenzyl compound of the formula (IV) wherein X is a leaving group, wherein the steps (a) and (b) are conducted in the presence of at least one substantially anhydrous inert organic solvent.
claims:
Claims Claims 1. A process for preparing (Â±)-2-exo-(2-Methylbenzyloxy)-1 -methyl-4-isopropyl-7-oxabicy- clo[2.2.1]heptane of the formula (I) any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof comprising the steps of (a) reacting (Â±)-2-exo-hydroxy-1-methyl-4-isopropyl-7-oxabicyclo[2.2.1]heptane of the formula (II) any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof with elemental sodium to form a sodium salt of the formula (III) any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof, and (b) reacting the sodium salt of the formula (III), any of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof with a 2-Methylbenzyl compound of the for- mula (IV) wherein X is a leaving group, wherein the steps (a) and (b) are conducted in the presence of at least one substan- tially anhydrous inert organic solvent. 2. The process according to claim 1 wherein the substantially anhydrous inert organic sol- vent is selected from aprotic organic solvents. 3. The process according to claim 1 or 2 wherein the substantially anhydrous inert organic solvent is selected from hydrocarbons, amides, ethers, ketones, nitriles, and any combi- nation thereof, preferably selected from hydrocarbons. 4. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 3 wherein the substantially anhydrous inert organic solvent is selected from aliphatic hydrocarbons, cycloaliphatic hydrocar- bons, aromatic hydrocarbons and any combination thereof, preferably selected from ali- phatic hydrocarbons, aromatic hydrocarbons and any combination thereof and more preferably selected from aromatic hydrocarbons. 5. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 4 wherein the substantially anhydrous inert organic solvent is selected from n-heptane, n-octane, cycloheptane, cyclooctane, toluene, o-xylene, m-xylene, p-xylene, ethylbenzene, 2-propylbenzene (cumene), 2-iso- propyltoluene (o-cymol), 3-isopropyltoluene (m-cymol), 4-isopropyltoluene (p-cymol), 1 ,3,5-trimethylbenzene (mesitylene) and any combination thereof, preferably selected from n-heptane, n-octane, toluene, o-xylene, m-xylene, p-xylene, ethylbenzene, 1 ,3,5- trimethylbenzene (mesitylene) and any combination thereof, more preferably selected from n-heptane, toluene, o-xylene, m-xylene, p-xylene and any combination thereof, even more preferably selected from toluene, o-xylene, m-xylene, p-xylene and any com- bination thereof and most preferably toluene. 6. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 5 wherein the elemental sodium used in step (a) is present in the form of liquid sodium, a dispersion of sodium particles in an inert dispersing medium or a combination thereof. 7. The process according to claim 6 wherein the inert dispersing medium of the dispersion of sodium particles is identical to the substantially anhydrous inert organic solvent used in steps (a) and (b). 8. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 7 wherein the substantially anhydrous inert organic solvent and/or the inert dispersing medium has a boiling point of equal to or more than the melting point of sodium (97.7 Â°C). 9. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 8 wherein the substantially anhydrous inert organic solvent has a water content lower than 5% by weight, preferably lower than 1 % by weight, more preferably lower than 0.5% by weight, even more preferably lower than 0.1 % by weight, still more preferably lower than 0.01 % by weight, yet more prefer ably lower than 0.001 % by weight and most preferably lower than 0.0001 % by weight (in each case based on the total weight of the substantially anhydrous inert organic sol- vent) 10. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 9 wherein the molar ratio of the ele- mental sodium (calculated as 100% Na) to (Â±)-2 -exo-hydroxy-1 -methyl-4-isopropyl-7- oxabicyclo[2.2.1]heptane of the formula (II), any one of its individual enantiomers or any non-racemic mixture thereof is from 0.95 : 1 to 5 : 1. 1 1. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 10 wherein the step (b) is conducted in the presence of at least one phase transfer catalyst. 12. The process according to claim 1 1 wherein the phase transfer catalyst is selected from quarternary ammonium salts, quarternary pyridinium salts, quarternary phosphonium salts and any combination thereof. 13. The process according to any one of claim 1 1 or 12 wherein the phase transfer catalyst is selected from quaternary ammonium salts of the general formula (VI) (R 4 R 5 R 6 R 7 N) + Zr (VI) wherein R 4 , R 5 , R 6 and R 7 are the same or different and are each independently se- lected from the group consisting of Ci-C2o-alkyl, C3-Cio-cycloalkyl, C6-C2o-aryl and Ce- C2o-aryl-Ci-C4-alkyl-, and Z- f is a monovalent anion, quarternary phosphonium salts of the general formula (VII) (R 8 R 9 R 10 R 11 p) + Z 2 - (VII) wherein R 8 , R 9 , R 10 and R 11 are the same or different and are each independently se- lected from a 5- or 6-membered heterocyclyl group containing 1 , 2 or 3 heteroatoms selected from the group consisting of O, N and S, and Zr is a monovalent anion, and any combination thereof. 14. The process according to any one of claims 1 1 to 13 wherein the phase transfer catalyst is selected from tetra-n-butylammonium chloride, tri-n-octylmethylammonium chloride, 

 n-dodecyltrimethylammonium chloride, benzyl(tri-n-butyl)ammonium chloride, tetrakis(1- piperidyl)phosphonium chloride and any combination thereof. 15. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 14 wherein X is halogen. 16. The process according to any one of claims 1 to 15 wherein the 2-Methylbenzyl corn- pound of the formula (IV) is 2-Methylbenzyl chloride of the formula (IVa)
